---
title: "Installing R Packages"
---

In R, you can install packages directly from within your script using the `install.packages()` function. For example, to install **ggplot2**, a popular package for data visualization, include the following line at the beginning of your script:
```R
install.packages("ggplot2")
```

Before you install a package, make sure to set up your R environment with **renv** to manage dependencies and ensure reproducibility. See our page on [renv](/pages/resources/envs/renv.qmd) for more details.

## Installing RStudio
To install RStudio, an interactive environment for writing R scripts, please refer to the [installation instructions](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/) on the RStudio site.
